title: Systems and methods for determining when to release control of an autonomous vehicle
abstract: Systems and methods for controlling an autonomous vehicle are provided. In one example embodiment, a computer-implemented method includes providing a control of the autonomous vehicle to a user associated with a vehicle service. The method includes identifying one or more release signals indicative of one or more actions of the user. The method includes releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user, based at least in part on the release signals.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A computer-implemented method for controlling an autonomous vehicle, the method comprising: providing, by a computing system comprising one or more computing devices, a control of the autonomous vehicle to a user associated with a vehicle service; identifying, by the computing system, one or more release signals indicative of one or more actions of the user; and releasing, by the computing system, the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user, based at least in part on the one or more release signals. 2. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, further comprising: receiving, by the computing system, data representing a starting location associated with the vehicle service; causing, by the computing system, the autonomous vehicle to travel in accordance with a first route that leads to the starting location; and providing, by the computing system, the control of the autonomous vehicle to the user when the autonomous vehicle is at the starting location. 3. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, further comprising: receiving, by the computing system, data representing an ending location associated with the vehicle service; causing, by the computing system, the autonomous vehicle to travel in accordance with a second route that leads to the ending location; and releasing, by the computing system, the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user when the vehicle is at the ending location. 4. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein identifying the one or more release signals comprises: obtaining, by the computing system, sensor data indicative of an interior environment of the autonomous vehicle; 

 obtaining, by the computing system, sensor data indicative of an exterior environment of the autonomous vehicle; and identifying, by the computing system, the one or more release signals based at least in part on the sensor data. 5. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user comprises: assigning, by the computing system, a weight to each of the one or more release signals; determining, by the computing system, a confidence score based on the one or more release signals and the assigned weights; and releasing, by the computing system, the control of the vehicle if the confidence score is greater than a threshold value. 6. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user comprises: receiving, by the computing system, an instruction to release the control; and releasing, by the computing system, the control of the autonomous vehicle in response to the instruction. 7. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein receiving an instruction to release the control comprises: sending, by the computing system, a request to the user to release the control; and receiving, by the computing system, the instruction from the user. 8. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein releasing the control of the vehicle comprises causing the autonomous vehicle to drive away. 9. A computing system for controlling an autonomous vehicle, the system comprising: one or more processors; and 

 one or more tangible, non-transitory, computer readable media that collectively store instructions that when executed by the one or more processors cause the computing system to perform operations, the operations comprising: providing a control of the autonomous vehicle to a user associated with a vehicle service; identifying one or more release signals indicative of one or more actions of the user; and releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user, based at least in part on the one or more release signals. 10. The computing system of any of the preceding claims, wherein the operations further comprise: receiving data representing a starting location associated with the vehicle service; causing the autonomous vehicle to travel in accordance with a first route that leads to the starting location; and providing the control of the autonomous vehicle to the user when the autonomous vehicle is at the starting location. 11. The computing system of any of the preceding claims, wherein the operations further comprise: receiving data representing an ending location associated with the vehicle service; causing the autonomous vehicle to travel in accordance with a second route that leads to the ending location; and releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user when the vehicle is at the ending location. 12. The computing system of any of the preceding claims, wherein determining the one or more release signals comprises: obtaining sensor data indicative of an interior environment of the autonomous vehicle; obtaining sensor data indicative of an exterior environment of the autonomous vehicle; and 

 identifying the one or more release signals based at least in part on the sensor data. 13. The computing system of any of the preceding claims, wherein releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user comprises: assigning a weight to each of the one or more release signals; determining a confidence score based on the one or more release signals and the assigned weights; and releasing the control of the vehicle if the confidence score is greater than a threshold value. 14. The computing system of any of the preceding claims, wherein releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user comprises: receiving an instruction to release the control; and releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle in response to the instruction. 15. An autonomous vehicle, comprising: one or more processors; and one or more tangible, non-transitory, computer readable media that collectively store instructions that when executed by the one or more processors cause the autonomous vehicle to perform operations, the operations comprising: providing a control of the autonomous vehicle to a user associated with a vehicle service; identifying one or more release signals indicative of one or more actions of the user; and releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user, based at least in part on the one or more release signals. 16. The autonomous vehicle of any of the preceding claims, wherein the operations further comprise: receiving data representing a starting location associated with the vehicle service; causing the autonomous vehicle to travel in accordance with a first route that leads to the starting location; and 

 providing the control of the autonomous vehicle to the user when the autonomous vehicle is at the starting location. 17. The autonomous vehicle of any of the preceding claims, wherein the operations further comprise: receiving data representing a ending location associated with the vehicle service; causing the autonomous vehicle to travel in accordance with a second route that leads to the ending location; and releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user when the vehicle is at the ending location. 18. The autonomous vehicle of any of the preceding claims, wherein determining the one or more release signals comprises: obtaining sensor data indicative of an interior environment of the autonomous vehicle; obtaining sensor data indicative of an exterior environment of the autonomous vehicle; and identifying the one or more release signals based at least in part on the sensor data. 19. The autonomous vehicle of any of the preceding claims, wherein releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user comprises: assigning a weight to each of the one or more release signals; determining a confidence score based on the one or more release signals and the assigned weights; and releasing the control of the vehicle if the confidence score is greater than a threshold value. 20. The autonomous vehicle of any of the preceding claims, wherein releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle from the user comprises: receiving an instruction to release the control; and releasing the control of the autonomous vehicle in response to the instruction.
